etymology.description: Hut (stylized as 穴) + mouth 口 = window opening
etymology.interpretation: The character 冏 (jiǒng) originally depicted a structure resembling a hut with an opening. This design, where the hut is stylized as 穴 (xuè, "cave") and the central 口 (kǒu, "mouth") suggests an opening, led to the concept of a window or ventilation. Historically, its usage broadened to serve as a phonetic component in other characters.
